untranslatable_reason: Signal requires cross-asset yield comparison (Asset A yield vs Asset B required return differential of 200+ bps) which cannot be expressed using single-instrument price/technical indicators. The rule specifically demands multi-asset relative value analysis across competing asset classes (e.g., Treasury notes vs equities), which requires external yield data not available in the ALLOWED INDICATORS list. The 10-day persistence confirmation and relative weakness assessment also require comparative asset metrics not provided.
translation_notes: This is a fundamental relative value / asset allocation signal, not a single-asset technical trading rule. It requires yield curve data, cross-asset spreads, and regime identification across multiple asset classes. The source quote confirms this is about Treasury note yields during a specific 1981-1982 interest rate regime. Cannot be meaningfully translated to technical indicators without access to underlying yield/return differential data between competing assets.
